title: Sound Waves Publishing and Media — Full App Build

requirements.text: Create a Motoko backend actor that stores and manages all core data: artist profiles (with eligibility status), share ownership records, event registrations, payment transactions, and accounting entries. Initialize the share structure with 10,000,000 total shares at $1.00 starting price: 7,000,000 pre-allocated to founder Mr. Robin T. Harding Smith (70%), and 3,000,000 available for artist purchase (30%).
acceptanceCriteria: Backend stores artist profiles with name, email, principal ID, submitted musical pieces count, and eligibility flag
Share ownership table records principal, shares owned, purchase price, and timestamp
Founder allocation of 7,000,000 shares is pre-seeded and immutable via normal artist purchase flows
3,000,000 shares are available for artist purchases
Current share price is stored and readable
Event registration records store attendee name, email, ticket quantity, payment status, and Stripe payment intent ID
Accounting/transaction log stores all financial events with type, amount, timestamp, and associated principal

requirements.text: Implement artist eligibility logic: an artist must submit 15 musical pieces to become eligible to purchase shares. Track the count of submitted pieces per artist profile. Once an artist reaches 15 submissions, mark them as eligible.
acceptanceCriteria: Artist profile tracks submitted_pieces count
Artists with submitted_pieces >= 15 are flagged as eligible
Share purchase calls reject ineligible artists with a clear error message
Eligibility status is queryable from the frontend

requirements.text: Implement share purchase logic: eligible artists can purchase between 1 and 7 shares at $1.00 each via Stripe. The first 100 eligible artists automatically receive 1 free share (no payment required for that share). Enforce a hard cap of 7 shares per artist across all purchases combined.
acceptanceCriteria: Artists can only buy between 1 and 7 shares total (lifetime cap)
Attempting to exceed 7 shares returns an error
The first 100 artists to qualify receive 1 free share credited automatically upon eligibility
Free share recipients tracked by a counter capped at 100
Share purchases beyond the free share go through Stripe payment
Available share pool decreases with each purchase; purchases fail if pool is exhausted

requirements.text: Integrate Stripe payment processing for both event ticket purchases and share purchases. Store Stripe secret key via an admin-only configuration endpoint. Create Stripe PaymentIntents for ticket and share purchases and return client secrets to the frontend. Implement a Stripe webhook endpoint to confirm payments and update registration/share ownership records accordingly.
acceptanceCriteria: Admin can configure Stripe secret key via a protected backend call
Backend creates PaymentIntents for ticket purchases (quantity × ticket price)
Backend creates PaymentIntents for share purchases (shares × $1.00)
Webhook endpoint receives Stripe events and marks transactions as confirmed
Share ownership is only finalized upon successful payment confirmation
Registration status updated to 'paid' upon webhook confirmation

requirements.text: Implement admin backend functions: query all shareholders with their share counts, query all event registrations, query full transaction/accounting log, and export data as CSV-formatted text. Admin access must be restricted to authorized principals.
acceptanceCriteria: Admin-only query returns all artist shareholders with name, email, shares owned, and total paid
Admin-only query returns all event registrations with attendee info and payment status
Admin-only query returns full accounting log
CSV export function returns a comma-separated string for shareholder and transaction data
Non-admin calls to admin functions return an authorization error

requirements.text: Build a public-facing home page for Sound Waves Publishing and Media with a hero section displaying the company name, founder attribution (Mr. Robin T. Harding Smith, Founder & Creator), and navigation links to Event Registration and Artist Share Purchase sections.
acceptanceCriteria: Home page displays 'Sound Waves Publishing and Media' as the primary heading
Founder credit 'Mr. Robin T. Harding Smith, Founder & Creator' is visible
Navigation links to Event Registration and Artist Portal pages
Page is responsive and visually polished

requirements.text: Build an Event Registration page with a form collecting attendee name, email, and ticket quantity. On submission, initiate a Stripe checkout flow using the PaymentIntent client secret returned from the backend. Show success and failure states after payment.
acceptanceCriteria: Form fields: full name, email address, ticket quantity (number input, min 1)
Order summary shows total price before payment
Stripe Elements card input embedded in the page
Successful payment shows confirmation screen with registration details
Payment failure shows error message with retry option

requirements.text: Build an Artist Portal page that allows artists to: (1) create/view their profile, (2) submit musical pieces to track toward the 15-piece eligibility requirement, (3) view their eligibility status, (4) view their current share count, and (5) purchase additional shares (up to their 7-share maximum) via Stripe if eligible.
acceptanceCriteria: Artists can log in via Internet Identity
Profile section shows name, email, submitted pieces count, and eligibility status
Button/form to submit a new musical piece (increments piece count)
Progress indicator shows pieces submitted out of 15 required
Share ownership section shows current shares held
Share purchase form (quantity selector 1–7 minus already owned) is visible only to eligible artists
First 100 eligible artists see a 'You qualify for 1 free share' notice
Stripe payment flow initiated for paid share purchases
Errors shown clearly if share cap is reached or eligibility not met

requirements.text: Build an Admin Dashboard page accessible only to admin principals. Display three tabs: (1) Shareholders — table of all artists with shares, share count, and total paid; (2) Event Registrations — table of all registrations with attendee info and payment status; (3) Accounting Log — full transaction history. Each tab must have a 'Download CSV' button that exports that tab's data.
acceptanceCriteria: Admin dashboard is only accessible to authenticated admin users
Shareholders tab lists artist name, email, shares owned, total paid, and free share status
Registrations tab lists attendee name, email, tickets, total paid, and payment status
Accounting tab lists transaction type, amount, date, and associated user
Each tab has a working CSV download button
Non-admin users are redirected or shown an access denied message

requirements.text: Apply a distinctive music-industry visual theme across the entire app: dark background with gold/amber accent colors, elegant serif or display typography for headings, and a professional layout that evokes a premium media company. Apply consistently across all pages.
acceptanceCriteria: Dark background (near-black or deep charcoal) used as base throughout
Gold or amber accent color used for buttons, highlights, and key UI elements
Heading typography uses an elegant display or serif font
Consistent color palette and spacing across Home, Event Registration, Artist Portal, and Admin pages
Theme is responsive and readable on mobile and desktop

constraints: All backend logic must remain in a single Motoko actor (backend/main.mo)
Stripe secret key must only be stored in the backend and never exposed to the frontend
Founder's 7,000,000 shares must never be purchasable or transferable via artist purchase flows
Share purchases must only be finalized after Stripe payment confirmation via webhook
Do not edit any files listed in frontend.immutablePaths
nonGoals: Share resale or secondary market trading between artists
External databases — all data stored in Motoko stable variables
Social login (Facebook, GitHub) — only Internet Identity supported
Real-time price fluctuation of shares (starting price is fixed at $1.00)

required: Sound Waves Publishing and Media logo — stylized sound wave graphic with musical note motif in gold on dark background (swpm-logo.dim_400x200.png)
Hero background — abstract dark studio atmosphere with subtle golden light rays and music waveform patterns (hero-bg.dim_1440x600.png)
